preamble: The transformation of the close-mid back rounded vowel /o/ into the diphthong /ui/ involves a complex series of phonetic changes. This shift typically includes raising of the vowel, fronting from a back to a front position, and diphthongization from a monophthong to a gliding vowel sound. Additionally, the process often entails unrounding, particularly in the final element of the diphthong.

examples.note: as part of the word /nit̪t̪o/ 'regularly' becoming /lit̪ui/

sources: Chatterjee, S. (2022). Phonological Analysis of Rampurhat Variety of Bangla. Language in India, 22(3), 95-108.
